MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT RICK IPPOLITO's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For The Production Of Medical Records Pertaining To Defendant.

Bradley White, Esq., Law Office of, (510) 219-5831, for Plaintiff.

Lori R. Mayfield, Esq., of FREEMAN MATHIS & GARY, LLP, (916) 272-1531, for Defendants.

Counsel for Plaintiff and Defendant stipulate to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem. Counsel and the judge pro tem (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation on their behalf.

The matter is argued. There is discussion regarding terms of a protective order. At conclusion of oral argument, the matter is taken under submission.

Counsel for Defendant is to prepare and submit a proposed order that tracks the tentative ruling and a proposed protective order which tracks the terms agreed upon during the hearing and email it to the judge pro tem at sborrowman@gmail.com.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Matter is not contested. Counsel for Defendant seeks clarification to the court's tentative ruling.

Hearing is held. Discussed, Not ARGUED.

DEFENDANTS IPPOLITO RENOVATIONS, INC.,, RICK IPPOLITO, ERICA IPPOLITO'S Motion To Quash

The Motion is denied in part / granted in part as follows:

The Motion to Quash Request Number 1 is granted to the extent it seeks documents unrelated to the issues in this case. For example, unrelated documents might include receipts for a box of nails or rental of a dumpster. The Motion to Quash is denied as to any responsive documents relating to workers compensation insurance, general insurance, contractor's licenses, and any contracts or subcontracts for work at 41 Mercedes Way.

The Motion to Quash Request Number 2 is denied.

No sanctions.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order, in word format, repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
